Project 1: Group Research Plan 

**Course:** KIN 7518 Social Issues in Sport 

**Due:** Monday, January 27, 2026 by 11:59 PM 

**Submission:** Email to yqian@lsu.edu (mailto:yqian@lsu.edu) — One member submits, **CC all group members Format:** Markdown (.md) file 

**File Naming:** GeauxTigers\_PLAN.md 

1\. Research Questions & Significance

RQ 1 

**The Question:** 

How does social media output affect an athlete’s image?

**The Context:** 

This question addresses public discourse, media studies, and the gap between an athlete’s brand and their perceived image. The chosen framework provides insight into the public’s perceptions of the athletes and why they get certain brand deals. For example, Angel Reese’s brand deals and public image revolves more around fashion and beauty products because that is most associated with the image that she puts out while Caitlin Clark has more deals that focus on her athletic performance. 

**Why It Matters:** 

Women’s basketball as a whole is becoming more popular due to this discourse. Professional and amateur women’s basketball is more successful with more fans and engagement, allowing for organizations to generate higher revenues and open more opportunities within the sport.

2\. Dataset Selection & Justification

**Dataset Choice:** Clark-Reese (ARCC) 

**Justification:** 

This data set is appropriate as it allows us to analyze the various forms of content both athletes release, the overall engagement with their content, and the reactions/perceived image their audiences have.

**Key files you plan to use:** ARCC\_INS\_COMMENT.xlsx/ARCC\_YT\_COMMENT.xlsx/ARCC\_YT\_VIDEO.xlsx/ARCC\_INS\_POST.xlsx

3\. Preliminary Variable Operationalization

**Construct Operational Definition Data Source / Indicator**

Sorting for skill-based comments, supportive/hateful comments, comments on body image, comparative comments

Keyword search: “layup”, “hate”, “love”, “rookie of the year”, “mebounds”, “ghetto”

4\. Proposed Analyses 

Outline the analytical approaches you plan to use. For each, explain how it addresses your RQ. 

We aim to analyze the data through sorting, key word frequency, the percentage of basketball vs non-basketball posts, and the percentage of negative/positive comments per post. Sorting and key word frequency will allow us to determine the emotional responses and consensus amongst viewers. The average negative/positive comments per post will allow us to observe the true quantitative results of the posts, detailing what the real majority response is from their respective audiences. Each of these forms of analysis will show us if the type of post is affecting each athlete’s image.

5\. Limitations & Potential Issues 

1\. There is no direct relationship between a social media post and the reaction from viewers. No specific type of post is guaranteed to provoke any certain responses.

2\. It is difficult to qualify sexually suggestive posts, as it is entirely subjective to the viewer.

3\. There may be a racial limitation that elicits different reactions from the same content.

6\. Ethical Considerations 

Address the following: 

**Privacy:** Are you analyzing public or private data? Any risks of identifying individuals? 

**Harm:** Could your analysis reinforce stereotypes or cause harm to groups discussed? 

**Bias:** What biases might exist in your data or your interpretation? 

**Privacy:** Because the purpose of this research study is to analyze public perceptions of athletes’ images, only publicly available social media content/data will be examined, as this is the content they voluntarily share on their social media platforms.

**Harm:** While we are aware that the analysis may reveal existing stereotypes associated with certain groups, the study itself is not intended to reinforce harmful narratives. Instead, we seek to examine how these stereotypes exist within the public perception and shaped by social media output. 

**Bias:** Biases may arise in both the data and from subjective interpretation based on personal preference, prior athletic success or existing narratives associated with each athlete.

7\. Group Role Assignments 

Antoine: Data Lead, data analysis and cleaning.

Areli: Theory Lead, literature integration and RQ refinement.

Ethan: Lead, Methods and tool implementation.

8\. Data Visualization Plan 

Create at least ONE data visualization that addresses your research question(s). This is a required deliverable due with your project plan. 

**Primary Goal:** 

What story does your visualization tell? What specific question does it answer? 

The visuals serve to highlight patterns in social media output and corresponding public perceptions analyzing how an athlete's image is constructed online.

**Visualization Description:** 

Describe what type of chart/graph you're creating and what it will show. (e.g., "Bar chart comparing frequency of X vs. Y," "Line graph showing change over time," "Word cloud of most common themes") 

This study will utilize comparative visualizations to analyze similarities and differences in social media output and audience engagement between Angel Reese and Caitlin Clark. Bar charts or frequency tables may be used to display the types of content posted (game-related, personal-life, appearance, brand partnerships, advocacy, etc.) 

**Design Rationale:** 

Why did you choose this visualization type? How does it clarify your argument or make your data more understandable? 

This visualization type was chosen to facilitate direct comparison between the athletes, making it easier to identify patterns in engagement and perception. Visualizing the data helps clarify how public responses may differ based on social media output, how it directly impacts brand partnerships and endorsements, as well as highlight and bring awareness to how public perception can be shaped by existing narratives and biases. 

**Verification Methods:** 

How will you ensure your visualization accurately represents your data? 

 We ensured that the visualization was accurate by spot-checking calculations against source data 

**The Visualization:** 

![][image1]

![][image2]

![][image3]

![][image4]

**Brief Interpretation (2-3 sentences):** 

Based on the analysis provided by the AI agent, it was determined that the platform certainly mattered when it came to the volume of comments on each athlete. There were more instagram comments than youtube comments, however the youtube comments did appear to produce more of a conversation among consumers rather than a quick remark. Further, the analysis found that across all platforms Angel Reese received more comments that were related to her appearance (17.7%) compared to Caitlin Clark (8.3%). This result was somewhat expected since most of Caitlin Clark’s media output is related to her basketball playing while Angel Reese has taken on more of an influencer role since she has been a star. 

9\. AI-Assisted Work Documentation & Verification 

For this plan and information provided we used Antigravity to analyze the data sets and provide visualizations. The first step was inputting our initial question into the Antigravity agent and telling the program to ask questions to clarify the analysis that we wanted. This included verifying the names of the athletes, the key words we wanted to focus on, and the types of visualization charts that we wanted it to create. 

**Tools Used:** 

The AI tool used for this analysis was Claude Sonnet 4.5. 

**Verification Methods:** 

We verified that the outputs were accurate by reviewing the number of comments provided in the graphs and compared them to the number of comments that we could see in the data set.

**Code Explanation:** 

There was no AI coding used for this assignment

**Learning Reflection:** 

Our biggest takeaway from working with AI on this project was that it has many more capabilities than we thought possible. It took a while for the results to be provided but in the end the results that it gave us were so detailed that it was worth it. The amount of data that the agent had to sift through was very impressive and it really made us think of how long it would have taken a human to go through it. 

Submission Checklist 

Before submitting, confirm: 

 All sections completed 

 RQs are specific, contextualized, and significance is justified 

 Dataset choice is confirmed 

 At least one construct is operationalized  
 Limitations and ethics are addressed honestly 

 All group members are assigned roles 

 Data visualization is included (Section 8\) 

 Visualization includes verification methods 

 If AI tools were used, Section 9 is completed with verification documentation 

**Submission Reminder:** 

**File name:** GROUPNAME\_PLAN.md (e.g., GeauxTigers\_PLAN.md) 

**Visualization file name:** GROUPNAME\_VISUAL.\[extension\] (e.g., GeauxTigers\_VISUAL.pdf) **Email to:** yqian@lsu.edu (mailto:yqian@lsu.edu) 

**CC:** All group members 

**Deadline:** Monday, January 27, 2026 by 11:59 PM
